should_trigger.text: Audit my local skill library and rank cleanup priority, duplicate candidates, and security risk.
Review these downloaded skills before I install them. I want prompt injection, token leakage, and risky shell execution flagged.
Inventory this skill directory, summarize what each skill does, estimate usage warmth, and recommend archive, backup, or delete direction.
Check this skill folder for hardcoded secrets, unsafe network fetches, and whether the stale ones should be archived.
I downloaded a batch of third-party skills. Do a safety scan and cleanup review before anything gets installed or run.
Scan my local skills and tell me which ones are active, warm, cold, duplicated, or risky before I clean the library.

should_not_trigger.text: Create a new skill from this workflow and package it for team reuse.
Review this Python repository for security issues.
Delete these old files from my Downloads folder.
Improve the description of this single skill.
Summarize the risks in this note without scanning any files.

near_neighbor.text: Create a skill that audits local skills for cleanup and security.
Refactor this existing skill and add trigger evals.
Run a generic security audit on this app, not on the skills library.
Clean this directory, but do not analyze skill purpose, usage, or security.
